FY26 Objectives

Why We're Building This

Athlete Safety
Equip operators with instant access to athlete data and incident tools so they can respond faster to critical situations on race day.
Edit Race Slot Allocation Incident Report Incident View PCR
Real-Time Communication
Connect on-site operators with remote teams instantly — through targeted notifications, in-app chat, and a unified command view across all active events.
Broadcasting 1.0 Chat Global Dashboard Broadcasting 2.0
Platform Scale
Build infrastructure that supports growth across hundreds of events worldwide — with reliable offline resilience and self-service tooling for field staff.
Slot Allocation Infrastructure Live Activities Kiosk

What We Shipped

Last Deliveries

Foundation — 2025
01
Home Page
The central hub of the app — giving staff a real-time overview of active races, live event status, and quick access to all key tools.
Done
Click to view screens
02
Account Page
Staff can manage their personal profile, role, and access credentials — ensuring the right people have the right permissions on race day.
Done
Click to view screens
03
Race Page
Gives operators a complete view of a specific event — including race details, course information, leaderboard, start times, and team directory.
Done
Click to view screens

Athlete Page
Provides staff with instant access to individual athlete profiles — including personal data, race history, medical information, and emergency contacts.
Done
Click to view screens

Chat & Live Communication

Done
Click to view screens

Home Page

The central hub of the ROC app — staff see what's happening right now, including live events with real-time weather, and a full list of upcoming races filtered by location, type, and series.

  • Live event card with real-time weather and race clock
  • Upcoming races with location, date, and category filters
  • Search across any race or athlete
  • Location-aware view — adapts to the staff member's region
  • Available on mobile and web

Account Page

Staff can view and edit their personal profile — name, contact details, country, and role assignment. Role selection (IRONMAN Staff, Medical Worker, Volunteer) controls what each user can access across the app.

  • Edit name, date of birth, email, and phone number
  • Role assignment: IRONMAN Staff, Medical Worker, Volunteer
  • Profile photo management
  • Password update
  • Available on mobile and web

Race Page

A complete operational view of any event — staff can access race details, live leaderboard, course maps with weather data, wave start times, and the full team directory, all in one place.

  • Race details: location, date, time, course type, qualifier status
  • Live leaderboard updated in real time during the event
  • Course overview with swim, bike, and run maps and weather data
  • Start times organized by wave, division, and age group
  • Team & Operation directory with role-based filtering

Athlete Page

Staff have instant access to every athlete's full profile — personal details, live race location on the map, medical history, emergency contacts, and registration data per event. Available on mobile and web.

  • Live racing status with real-time location map
  • Personal data: age group, team, contact details, country
  • Medical information including pre-existing conditions
  • Emergency contact with one-tap calling
  • Registration history per event with snapshot data

Chat & Live Communication

Real-time messaging between all staff roles — IRONMAN Staff, Medical Workers, and Volunteers — scoped to each race. Staff can send direct messages, create group chats, forward messages to specific team members, and share images. Available on mobile and web.

  • Direct and group messaging scoped to each race
  • Role-based contact list: Staff, Medical Workers, Volunteers
  • Image and file sharing within conversations
  • Forward messages to specific team members
  • Unread badge and message search
Current Cycle — 2026
01
Slot Allocation
Manage athlete world-championship slot assignments with accept/decline workflow, age-graded ranking, and tap-to-pay processing for on-site registration.
Done Pending Store Release
Click to view screens
02
Edit Race
Gives coordinators direct control over race configurations — including location, qualifier settings, and attached documents — all updatable in real time from the app.
Concluding Finalizing Integrations
Click to view screens
03
Broadcasting 1.0
Send push and in-chat notifications from the web or app to all staff members. Messages are received directly on the app by targeted audience groups.
In Development
Click to view screens

Slot Allocation

Staff can view slot offers ranked by age-graded performance, accept or decline on behalf of athletes, and process registration with tap-to-pay. The full athlete list shows division rank, original time, age-graded time, and current status.

  • Current slot offer card with accept / decline actions
  • Complete athlete list with division rank and age-graded time
  • Status tracking: Waiting, Accepted, Passed, Failed
  • Athlete information pre-filled from previous registration
  • Secure payment redirect flow
ⓘ  Feature complete — pending approval for App Store and Play Store release.

Edit Race

Coordinators can update any race configuration field directly from the app — including race name, location with map search, date, status, course type per discipline, qualifier settings, documents, and wave start times by category group.

  • Location search with address autocomplete
  • Map picker for precise venue pinning
  • Qualifier toggle with independent date field
  • Document attachment management

Broadcasting 1.0

Staff can create and publish notifications from the web or the app. Messages are sent as push notifications or in-chat messages and received directly on the app by targeted audience groups such as Medical Workers, IRONMAN Staff, and Volunteers.

  • Notification type: Push or In-Chat
  • Title, message body, and optional destination link
  • Optional media attachment (up to 10MB)
  • Audience targeting by role group
  • iOS and Android preview before publishing
  • Estimated recipient count with opt-out awareness
Field Validation

Live Testing

TestFlight · Direct Link
Distribution — Not listed on App Store or Play Store
LogRocket
Monitoring — Session replay · Error tracking · Feedback

Monitoring & Feedback

LogRocket is fully implemented across the app — session replay, error tracking, and user feedback tools are all active. When field testing begins, the team will have full visibility into how staff use the app in real race-day conditions without manual data collection.

Field testing dates and event selection are TBD, pending alignment. This is an open item that requires a decision on target event, staff cohort, and success criteria before testing can begin.

LogRocket Implemented
Session Replay Active
Error Tracking Active
Field Testing — TBD, Pending Alignment
Up Next

What's Coming

The next cycle focuses on situational awareness and incident coordination — the operational core of race-day ROC management.

Design · Web

Global Dashboard

Multi-race operational overview for ROC supervisors — one interface covering all ongoing events, live incident feeds, athlete counts, and a global race map.

  • Gives ROC supervisors a single view of all active races — no more switching between systems during race day
  • Live incident feed with dispatch shortcuts, race clock, and weather per event
  • Global race map and directory covering all upcoming and finished events
Design · Web · Mobile

Incident Management

End-to-end incident lifecycle from field report to command center resolution, with live course map and full athlete medical context.

  • Reduces incident response time — field staff report on mobile, coordinators manage on the web command center with a live course map
  • Full athlete medical context at every step, from first report to resolution
  • Real-time resource tracking: medics, ambulances, helicopters, and vehicles per race
Planning · Under Construction

PCR · Live Activities · Kiosk

Three features in scoping. PCR enables full medical documentation from QR scan to discharge. Live Activities and Kiosk are in early planning.

  • PCR replaces paper-based medical records on race day — full documentation from QR scan to discharge, tied to the athlete profile
  • Live Activities and Kiosk extend the platform to iOS/Android widgets and on-site self-service — both in early scoping
